item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: God, this was such a fun article to read, especially the way the statcraft is made. I would have loved it more if you changed the aliens in ST a bit more representable rather than the stupid "humanoid-ancestor" crap. I was about to upload a bunch of pics to Youtube and deviantart to show the physiological and meta-phonological properties of the Vulcans, Klingons, Cardassians, Andronians, ect. but Roddenbury was a good universe creator yet had horrifically bigoted writers on board to sterotype humanity. I would justify the Gene is somewhere in Writers Limbo where he is still taking praiss and disgust from his creation.

item.title: I love this
item.content:encoded:
I bet you weren't expecting a new comment on this. I actually remember reading this back then and I loved it.

I do wonder, though. What if people somehow managed to break into the fake Enterprise? Will they become part of the TV show or will it break the SCP and bad stuff happens?

item.title: awesome article
item.content:encoded: So I guess what happens is whatever goes on in SCP-3135-2's head happens in SCP-3135-3. That's my theory, although it could be wrong.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like if it was in space the SCP would lose what makes it an SCP and it would just become the starship enterprise.

item.title: Picking On Poor Wesley
item.content:encoded:
Given the humour in the article, I presume it's a dig at Wesley Crusher not being a particular fan favourite character. And yes, he could be annoying at times, But he still saved lives like the rest of the crew.

Of course a proper (ST:TNG) canon explanation for Wesley's absence is that, as this is still the Enterprise-D, Wesley is obviously still on his journey with The Traveller that he first started in "Journeys End".

Neelix is probably just another joke.

Another character that didn't get the respect they deserved. At least the Voyager Crew did eventually start taking the EMH seriously. Possibly because they eventually realised he was their only source of proper medical treatment in the Delta Quadrant.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is the skip we deserve. I like this on numerous levels.

Okay, but what did they do to Wesley? D: And why Neelix? XD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I dig it, but I'm also a total space-opera/sci-fi nerd, so I'm not exactly an objective reader. Once the original "director" died, did the Foundation start conducting more experiments to understand his implants better? I'd get a kick out of learning what they find out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It helps if you know the show, but I feel like it stands on it's own merits. I think the only drawback is the fact it's stuck underground and not actually in space.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Made some minor wording changes based on your suggestions, also

+ Regarding the John Luke Picard thing and the increase in nudity, graphic violence, and profanity.
- Close

While the fanboy is the one directly creating the fake Enterprise and directing the entities,
the people who put him in there also have control over The Journey Continues! So they're also making changes based on whatever they think will make the series more accessible and exciting to whoever their audience is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

Hesitant upvote. I feel like there's a lot more potential here that you didn't capitalize on, but maybe that's my Next Generation fanboy just wanting more. So I guess for the most part, it's short, sweet, and imaginative.

+ show block
– hide block

Nitpicks, questions, suggestions:

Blasts of light have also been observed originating from SCP-3135-1, along with the launching of several torpedoes.

"Torpedoes" is the Star Trek word for them, but I'm not sure the SCP foundation would call them such. The foundation might just call them projectiles, especially in a technical document like this. Same reason you have them refer to "phasers" as "blasts of light."

These organisms appear to behave consistently with the portrayal of the Enterprise's crew in the original series…

Because The Original Series is essentially a Star Trek proper noun for the 1966 series, you should consider removing the word "original" or changing it to "The Next Generation" or "the original run of the series."

There is a drastic increase in nudity, graphic violence, and profanity. The majority of said profanity can be attributed to the character of Data, who uses the word 'fuck' a minimum of ten times per episode.

What's the reason for this? The fan author is trying to make it "edgy?" Why Data, the unfeeling android?

The character Jean Luc Picard's name is now John Luke Picard.

What's the reason for this? An error on the fanboy's part? I feel like all trekkies can spell Jean-Luc Picard's name. If you're trying to show that the fanboy writing this can't spell someone's name, I would make it something -slightly- less well known. (Maybe Deanna Troi is Deanna Troy?)

The majority of episodes are now based around the character William Riker and his various sexual encounters.

I like this, because you're really getting at the crux of the concept here, which is that this is a fan work. You might add another bullet about how there are a lot more action scenes than in the original series, and that one character (Riker? Picard?) is constantly portrayed as a hero for his bravery and fighting ability or something.

Thanks for writing this, and I look forward to reading more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel as though you're half way there. There's enough to separate this from similar concepts, but I wouldn't say there's enough to make this unique. Perhaps its because of the Star Trek parody on Black Mirror or maybe just because I never watched Star Trek, but this just struck me as underwhelming. Not bad, but not particularly good either. No vote for now.

item.title: Great idea, great execution.
item.content:encoded: Inb4 all televised programs are perfectly recreated to the last molecule by aliens for their own entertainment.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Engage!

Thanks to Ensigns TheGreatSea, DarkStuff, Westrin, Stallmantic, magnadeus and CaptainKirby for their feedback in chat!
